external_references.description: An, J and Malhotra, A. (2021, November 10). North Korean attackers use malicious blogs to deliver malware to high-profile South Korean targets. Retrieved December 29, 2021.
Tarakanov , D.. (2013, September 11). The “Kimsuky” Operation: A North Korean APT?. Retrieved August 13, 2019.
Varadharajan Krishnasamy, Aditya K Sood. (2025, July 29). From Reconnaissance  to Control: The Operational Blueprint of Kimsuky APT  for Cyber Espionage. Retrieved April 18, 2026.

objects.description: [Kimsuky](https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0094) has collected Office, PDF, and HWP documents from its victims.(Citation: Securelist Kimsuky Sept 2013)(Citation: Talos Kimsuky Nov 2021) [Kimsuky](https://attack.mitre.org/groups/G0094) has also harvested victim files through the use of the `RecentFiles()` function that collects paths of recently accessed files by parsing .lnk shortcuts from `%APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\Recent`.(Citation: Aryaka Kimsuky July 2025)
